test_cases.description: Art-house documentary — Sicilian salt harvester — IDFA/CPH:DOX/Hot Docs selections, award, two critical quotes
test_cases.input_summary: 82-min film, 3 festival selections, 1 award, 2 critical quotes from named publications, Italian production, minimal-dialogue film
test_cases.output_summary: Clean one-page structure: tagline 'The last harvest.' captures subject and stakes in 3 words; film description distinguishes from generic landscape docs; festival block correctly leads with award; credits clean and complete

test_cases.description: Investigative documentary — corporate malfeasance — no festival history yet, pre-release
test_cases.input_summary: 75-min film, strong subject, no festival selections yet, strong critical position, seeking distributor before festival run
test_cases.output_summary: One-sheet with flagged note that pre-festival documents are weaker; description and tagline carry full weight; Claude flags that adding festival credits after first selection will significantly strengthen the document

test_cases.description: Sports documentary — marathon runner — broad theatrical/streaming audience, multiple awards, extensive festival history
test_cases.input_summary: 90-min film, 8 festival selections, 3 awards, 4 critical quotes, US production, sales agent attached
test_cases.output_summary: Awards and selections prioritized intelligently (top-tier first), quotes curated down to 2 strongest, tagline skews emotional for broad audience rather than art-house register

notes: Highest-scoring skill in the business batch. The structured one-sheet format is well-suited to Claude's output strengths. Tagline quality is the most variable element — depends on input richness. Actionability slightly lower because design execution is outside scope.
